rules.summary: Before editing a file in a subdirectory, read every README.md on the path from the repository root through the target directory.
rules.trigger_intent: edit an existing file in a repository subdirectory
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.summary: Before creating a file in a subdirectory, read every README.md on the path from the repository root through the target directory.
rules.trigger_intent: create a new file in a repository subdirectory
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.summary: When changing Next.js source or integration tests, start the Next.js watch-mode dev process before editing unless it is already running, or explicitly explain why it was skipped.
rules.trigger_intent: edit Next.js source code or an integration test
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running).

rules.summary: When creating Next.js source or integration-test files, start the Next.js watch-mode dev process first unless it is already running, or explicitly explain why it was skipped.
rules.trigger_intent: create a Next.js source file or integration-test file
rules.evidence: If you skip this, explicitly state why (for example: docs-only, read-only investigation, or CI-only analysis).

rules.summary: Keep the Next.js watch build running while iterating on code.
rules.trigger_intent: run iterative development commands after starting the Next.js watch build
rules.evidence: Keep this running while you iterate on code

rules.summary: Use the focused test script matching the development/production mode and Turbopack/Webpack bundler being verified.
rules.trigger_intent: run a focused Next.js integration test
rules.evidence: Run focused tests with the matching mode script:

rules.summary: After finishing, terminate the background watch process if you started it.
rules.trigger_intent: finish an iterative development session that started a background watch process
rules.evidence: When done, kill the background watch process (if you started it).

rules.summary: For type errors only, run the fast types command instead of building Next.js.
rules.trigger_intent: check only for type errors
rules.evidence: Use `pnpm --filter=next types` (~10s) instead of `pnpm --filter=next build` (~60s).

rules.summary: After bootstrap, prefer the filtered Next.js build when edits are limited to core Next.js files.
rules.trigger_intent: build after edits limited to core Next.js files in an already-bootstrapped workspace
rules.evidence: prefer `pnpm --filter=next build` when edits are limited to core Next.js files

rules.summary: Run the full build for branch switches or bootstrap, before a CI push, or when changes span packages.
rules.trigger_intent: build after switching branches or bootstrapping, before pushing to CI, or after changing multiple packages
rules.evidence: Use full `pnpm build-all` for branch switches/bootstrap, before CI push, or when changes span multiple packages.

rules.summary: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches.
rules.trigger_intent: switch git branches
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches:

rules.summary: Use --webpack to force Webpack for next build or next dev.
rules.trigger_intent: run next build or next dev with Webpack rather than the default Turbopack
rules.evidence: To force webpack:

rules.summary: Do not pass the nonexistent --no-turbopack flag.
rules.trigger_intent: disable Turbopack for a Next.js command
rules.evidence: There is no `--no-turbopack` flag.

rules.summary: Generate every new test using pnpm new-test.
rules.trigger_intent: generate a new test file or test suite
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.summary: Do not manually create a new test suite; use pnpm new-test so its structure and fixture files come from the template.
rules.trigger_intent: create a new test suite file
rules.evidence: Use `pnpm new-test` to generate new test suites

rules.summary: AI agents should generate tests non-interactively by forwarding --args in the documented appDir/name/type format.
rules.trigger_intent: run pnpm new-test as an AI coding agent
rules.evidence: Use --args for non-interactive mode (forward args to the script using `--`)

rules.summary: Never rerun the same test suite merely with different grep filters; capture its full output once and analyze the saved file.
rules.trigger_intent: analyze test output using multiple grep filters
rules.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it:

rules.summary: In tests, wait by polling with retry() from next-test-utils instead of setTimeout.
rules.trigger_intent: edit test code that waits for an asynchronous condition
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting

rules.summary: Do not use deprecated check(); use retry() with expect() instead.
rules.trigger_intent: edit test code that polls or checks for an expected value
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.summary: Prefer real fixture directories over inline files objects in tests.
rules.trigger_intent: edit nextTestSetup fixture configuration in a test
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.summary: When asked about PR status, reviews, or CI failures, run scripts/pr-status.js, optionally with the requested PR number.
rules.trigger_intent: investigate CI failures, PR reviews, or the status of a pull request
rules.evidence: When the user asks about CI failures, PR reviews, or the status of a PR, run the pr-status script:

rules.summary: In PR triage, prioritize blocking failures in build, lint, types, then tests order.
rules.trigger_intent: choose which CI failure to investigate or reproduce first
rules.evidence: Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests.

rules.summary: Treat CI failures as real until disproven and do not automatically dismiss failures listed as known flaky tests.
rules.trigger_intent: triage or decide whether to reproduce a CI failure
rules.evidence: Assume failures are real until disproven; use "Known Flaky Tests" as context, not auto-dismissal.

rules.summary: Reproduce failures with the same CI mode and environment variables, especially IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1 when present.
rules.trigger_intent: run a local reproduction of a CI failure
rules.evidence: Reproduce with the same CI mode/env vars (especially `IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1` when present).

rules.summary: For module-resolution or build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command so package resolution is exercised.
rules.trigger_intent: test a module-resolution or build-graph fix
rules.evidence: For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command so package resolution is exercised.

rules.summary: Use the pr-status-triage skill for automated PR or CI analysis.
rules.trigger_intent: perform automated PR status or CI failure analysis
rules.evidence: Use `$pr-status-triage` for automated analysis

rules.summary: Prioritize CI failures over review comments during PR analysis.
rules.trigger_intent: choose between investigating CI failures and addressing PR review comments
rules.evidence: Prioritize CI failures over review comments

rules.summary: For a rust check/build CI failure, run cargo fmt -- --check, then cargo fmt.
rules.trigger_intent: triage a rust check or rust build CI failure
rules.evidence: `rust check / build` → Run `cargo fmt -- --check`, then `cargo fmt`

rules.summary: For a lint/build Prettier failure, run pnpm prettier --write on the affected file.
rules.trigger_intent: fix a Prettier error reported by the lint/build CI job
rules.evidence: `lint / build` → Run `pnpm prettier --write <file>` for prettier errors

rules.summary: For a test failure, run the specific failing test path locally.
rules.trigger_intent: reproduce a test failure from CI
rules.evidence: test failures → Run the specific failing test path locally

rules.summary: Run tests in the development or production script matching the failing mode.
rules.trigger_intent: choose a command to reproduce a mode-specific test failure
rules.evidence: Run tests in the right mode:

rules.summary: Every PR description must end with the NEXT_JS_LLM_PR HTML comment.
rules.trigger_intent: create or update a pull request description
rules.evidence: When writing PR descriptions, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description:

rules.summary: Use DEBUG=next:* when enabling Next.js debug logging.
rules.trigger_intent: run Next.js with debug logging enabled
rules.evidence: Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging

rules.summary: Set NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 when testing locally.
rules.trigger_intent: run tests locally
rules.evidence: Use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally

rules.summary: Use NODE_ENV != production only for code that belongs in development bundles but must be eliminated from production bundles.
rules.trigger_intent: edit a build-time environment guard for code included in development bundles
rules.evidence: code that should exist in dev bundles but be eliminated from prod bundles. This is a build-time check.

rules.summary: Use __NEXT_DEV_SERVER for code that should run only under next dev, not debug-prerender builds or next start.
rules.trigger_intent: edit an environment guard for code that should execute only in the development server
rules.evidence: code that should only run with the dev server (`next dev`), not during `next build --debug-prerender` or `next start`.

rules.summary: Treat environment variable values as sensitive unless they are known test-mode flags.
rules.trigger_intent: run a command that accesses, expands, logs, or forwards environment variable values
rules.evidence: Always treat environment variable values as sensitive unless they are known test-mode flags.

rules.summary: Never print or paste secret values into commits or shared logs.
rules.trigger_intent: run a command that prints logs, creates a commit, or otherwise emits secret values
rules.evidence: Never print or paste secret values (tokens, API keys, cookies) in chat responses, commits, or shared logs.

rules.summary: Mirror CI environment names and modes exactly, without putting literal secret values into commands.
rules.trigger_intent: construct a local command reproducing CI environment settings
rules.evidence: Mirror CI env **names and modes** exactly, but do not inline literal secret values in commands.

rules.summary: If a required secret is unavailable locally, stop and ask the user instead of inventing placeholder credentials.
rules.trigger_intent: run a command that requires a secret missing from the local environment
rules.evidence: If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials.
